raw_text: The tin is cute and holds the card perfectly. I love that it costs nothing additional to add the tin. Delivery was fast and balance was accurate.  My only concern is that the gift card itself is somewhat ... paper-ish and feels like cardboard. If I didn't know better, I would have thought the gift card was a paper "placeholder" and I would have thrown it out inadvertently (similar to the placeholder stock photos you get when you buy a new picture frame). Thankfully I looked at the back of the card and realized the cardboard cutout was actually a gift card.

raw_text: I've gotten Amazon gift cards in tins for years because they make the gift seem much nicer.  I'm generally happy with them, but I really don't like that you can't see the value.  If you get multiple cards in the same tin with different values, there's no easy way to tell which is which.  Once I got one on which the paint was so scuffed up I didn't feel comfortable giving it as a gift, but Amazon wasn't willing to replace the tin.  I'm guessing the tins are all prefilled with cards, so they couldn't do that, but it was disappointing.  Fortunately, shipping is free, and I had a few more days before I needed to give the card, so I was able to order a second one and use the original gift card myself.
